title: Build a Traffic Light
subtitle: Light up red, yellow, and green LEDs in sequence.

hints.content: Each LED needs its own resistor and its own Arduino pin.
Wire pin 11 → resistor → red LED → GND, pin 12 → resistor → yellow LED → GND, pin 13 → resistor → green LED → GND.
digitalWrite(11,HIGH); delay(2000); digitalWrite(11,LOW); digitalWrite(12,HIGH); delay(1500); digitalWrite(12,LOW); digitalWrite(13,HIGH); delay(2000); digitalWrite(13,LOW);

narrative.briefing: Real traffic lights cycle through three colors on a timer. Wire three LEDs to three pins and sequence them: red, yellow, green, repeat.
narrative.context: Each LED is electrically independent — they each need their own resistor and their own digital pin. Sharing one resistor across LEDs would let current flow unpredictably between them.
narrative.successMessage: All three lights worked! That's the same logic real traffic signals use.
